channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Most K-12 teachers say AI's impact on education will eclipse the internet or computers
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.content:encoded: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
item.description: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: What do you actually get when you pay for AI?
item.description: Just 3% of U.S. households pay for AI for personal use. Sign ups are growing — even though Americans have subscription fatigue.
item.content:encoded:
Just 3% of U.S. households pay for AI for personal use. Sign ups are growing — even though Americans have subscription fatigue.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Thieves are targeting the world's copper. This phone company is fighting back
item.description: The value of copper is rising, and thieves can make money by stripping it from phone poles, streetlights and EV chargers. But those thefts cost the rest of us.
item.content:encoded:
The value of copper is rising, and thieves can make money by stripping it from phone poles, streetlights and EV chargers. But those thefts cost the rest of us.

(Image credit: John Ruwitch/NPR)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Trump signs order requesting AI companies submit products for government review
item.description: President Trump is asking artificial intelligence firms to submit new models for government review. NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Alondra Nelson, who worked on AI policy in the Biden administration.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is asking artificial intelligence firms to submit new models for government review. NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Alondra Nelson, who worked on AI policy in the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: DOJ is investigating former congressman George Santos for insider trading on Kalshi
item.description: The disgraced former congressman allegedly bet on whether he would appear at the State of the Union address, prompting federal investigations.
item.content:encoded:
The disgraced former congressman allegedly bet on whether he would appear at the State of the Union address, prompting federal investigations.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Trump signs AI safety order seeking voluntary review of new models
item.description: The order asks AI companies to voluntarily submit their most powerful models for the government to test up to 30 days before releasing them to the public.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

The order asks AI companies to voluntarily submit their most powerful models for the government to test up to 30 days before releasing them to the public.

(Image credit: Graeme Sloan)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over alleged safety lapses
item.description: The lawsuit accuses the company of failing to warn users that ChatGPT could be dangerous and instead marketing it as safe and reliable.
item.content:encoded:
The lawsuit accuses the company of failing to warn users that ChatGPT could be dangerous and instead marketing it as safe and reliable.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: AI giant Anthropic prepares to sell stock to the public; files preliminary IPO paperwork
item.description: The Anthropic IPO, and those of other AI-related firms like OpenAI, could be among the biggest in U.S. history.
item.content:encoded:
The Anthropic IPO, and those of other AI-related firms like OpenAI, could be among the biggest in U.S. history.

(Image credit: RICCARDO MILANI/Hans Lucas/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: These AI models are free, private, and will never say 'no'
item.description: Open-weight AI models with advanced capabilities and no safeguards are becoming much more accessible. While they can be useful, AI safety experts have concerns.
item.content:encoded:
Open-weight AI models with advanced capabilities and no safeguards are becoming much more accessible. While they can be useful, AI safety experts have concerns.

(Image credit: Patrick Lux)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan
